summary: The input UI exposes a change stream that emits the current LaTeX source string on every user edit. The output/error wiring subscribes and re-renders on each emission.

subscription_options.detail: The input element fires the standard 'input' event on every user edit; consumer reads element.value.
subscription_options.consumer_usage_example: inputEl.addEventListener('input', () => { const latex = inputEl.value; /* call renderer */ });

subscription_options.detail: The input UI module exposes a function such as onLatexChange(callback) that invokes callback(currentValue) on every user edit.

interface.selection_rule: Builders choose ONE form and document it. Either form satisfies the contract; the consumer must adapt to whichever is provided.

discovery.input_element_selector: [data-role='latex-input'] (or documented #id) — single, stable element on the page.
discovery.module_export: If using callback registration form, the symbol must be reachable from a known global or module export documented at integration time.

emission_semantics.fires_on: Every user-driven change to the textarea value (keystroke, paste, cut).
emission_semantics.payload: The current full string value of the textarea at the moment of emission.
emission_semantics.throttling: None required at this contract level. If builders add debouncing, the contract still requires that the LAST emission after a quiescent period reflect the final value.

data_passed: Output: the current LaTeX string — a plain string, not a DOM node, not an event object.
non_goals: The input UI does not call the renderer.
The input UI does not validate LaTeX.
The consumer does not write to the input element.
